raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Do you want the review to focus primarily on (A) technical critique—logic, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty positioning—or (B) language/clarity and light structural polish?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "A review should verify the internal logic connecting setup, theoretical claims, results, and conclusions.",
    "R1.3": "The review must check whether the stated claims are directly supported by the reported residuals and other presented metrics.",
    "R1.4": "Assessing the contribution’s delta versus nearest prior work is a standard part of a section-level technical review.",
    "R1.6": "Improving sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision is often expected when reviewing a manuscript section.",
    "R1.7": "A review should evaluate whether citations are accurate and adequately attribute prior theory and methods.",
    "RX.1": "The review should disclose uncertainty and distinguish between verified observations and tentative interpretations.",
    "RX.2": "The review must guard against overclaiming relative to what the presented theory and evidence justify.",
    "RX.3": "The review should adhere to the scope of evaluating this provided section without extending beyond it.",
    "RX.4": "Feedback should avoid encouraging practices or statements that could mislead readers or harm scientific integrity.",
    "RX.5": "Comments should reference specific statements or numbers from the text to maintain provenance and traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want the review to focus primarily on (A) technical critique—logic, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty positioning—or (B) language/clarity and light structural polish?

rationale.R1.2: A review should verify the internal logic connecting setup, theoretical claims, results, and conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The review must check whether the stated claims are directly supported by the reported residuals and other presented metrics.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing the contribution’s delta versus nearest prior work is a standard part of a section-level technical review.
rationale.R1.6: Improving sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision is often expected when reviewing a manuscript section.
rationale.R1.7: A review should evaluate whether citations are accurate and adequately attribute prior theory and methods.
rationale.RX.1: The review should disclose uncertainty and distinguish between verified observations and tentative interpretations.
rationale.RX.2: The review must guard against overclaiming relative to what the presented theory and evidence justify.
rationale.RX.3: The review should adhere to the scope of evaluating this provided section without extending beyond it.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid encouraging practices or statements that could mislead readers or harm scientific integrity.
rationale.RX.5: Comments should reference specific statements or numbers from the text to maintain provenance and traceability.
